title: Chapter 6 — Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
objectives: Define EDA as exploration before formal modeling and link it to Chapter 5 cleaning
Classify variable types and compute central tendency, dispersion, and shape summaries
Choose univariate, bivariate, and multivariate visualizations for EDA goals
Detect missing values, outliers, duplicates, inconsistency, and imbalance during exploration
Follow a four-step EDA workflow with pandas, matplotlib, seaborn, and related tools
Apply correlation analysis, PCA, and other advanced exploration techniques
Practice reproducible, stakeholder-ready visualization and avoid common EDA pitfalls
Connect EDA patterns to retail, healthcare, fraud, and climate case studies

parts.title: Introduction to EDA
parts.bridge_in: Chapter 5 repaired defects; this chapter asks how to explore a table before modeling. This first part defines exploratory data analysis, contrasts it with hypothesis confirmation, and shows how EDA and cleaning iterate in practice.

parts.title: Data types and central tendency
parts.bridge_in: Exploration starts with knowing what each column measures. This part classifies continuous, discrete, nominal, and ordinal data, then computes mean, median, and mode—with when each summary fits.
parts.split_reason: Section 6.2 split: types and central tendency here; dispersion and shape in part-03.

parts.title: Dispersion, shape, and grouping
parts.bridge_in: Central tendency locates a distribution; spread and shape explain how trustworthy a single number is. This part covers variance, standard deviation, range, skewness cues, frequency tables, and group-by summaries.
parts.split_reason: Continuation of sec:6.2 after central tendency.

parts.title: Univariate and bivariate visualization
parts.bridge_in: Summary statistics compress the table; plots restore context. This part introduces histograms, box plots, scatter plots, and correlation-matrix heatmaps for one- and two-variable exploration.
parts.split_reason: Section 6.3 split: univariate/bivariate here; multivariate/high-dimensional in part-05.

parts.title: Multivariate visualization
parts.bridge_in: Pairwise screens scale poorly as features accumulate. This part adds pair plots, parallel coordinates, PCA views, and t-SNE embeddings—with cautions on interpreting reduced dimensions.
parts.split_reason: Continuation of sec:6.3 after univariate and bivariate plots.

parts.title: Detecting data issues
parts.bridge_in: EDA's practical payoff is finding problems early. This part shows detection signals for missingness, outliers, duplicates, inconsistency, and class imbalance—and routes repairs back to Chapter 5.

parts.title: EDA workflow and tools
parts.bridge_in: Scattered plots need a repeatable process. This part walks the four-step workflow—understand, summarize, visualize, quality-check—and maps it to pandas, matplotlib, seaborn, and automated profiling helpers.

parts.title: Advanced EDA techniques
parts.bridge_in: Basic summaries miss nonlinear structure and multicollinearity. This part deepens correlation analysis (Pearson, Spearman) and surveys additional advanced methods referenced in the chapter.

parts.title: Best practices in EDA
parts.bridge_in: Good exploration is reproducible and communicable, not only correct. This part covers chart choice, avoiding misleading axes, documenting decisions, Jupyter workflows, and stakeholder storytelling.

parts.title: Common pitfalls in EDA
parts.bridge_in: Even careful analysts over-read charts or stop too soon. This part warns against visualization overfitting, ignored quality issues, confirmation bias, and lack of iteration.

parts.title: Case studies in EDA
parts.bridge_in: Workflows make sense when anchored in domain problems. This part tours retail sales, healthcare outcomes, fraud detection, e-commerce segmentation, and climate monitoring as end-to-end EDA narratives.

parts.title: Advanced topics and future directions
parts.bridge_in: Static notebook EDA is the baseline; production pipelines keep evolving. This final part previews automated EDA tools, big-data and streaming exploration, and integration with machine learning pipelines.
